Bash(python - <<'EOF'
from PIL import Image

src = r"C:\\Users\\Joe\\Desktop\\powermeter-demo\\powermeter-demo\\src\\assets\\logo.png"
out = r"C:\\Users\\Joe\\Desktop\\powermeter-demo\\powermeter-demo\\src\\assets\\logo_square.png"

img = Image.open\(src\).convert\("RGBA"\)
w, h = img.size
size = max\(w, h\)

square = Image.new\("RGBA", \(size, size\), \(255, 255, 255, 0\)\)
square.paste\(img, \(\(size - w\) // 2, \(size - h\) // 2\)\)
square.save\(out\)
print\(f"Saved {size}x{size} square to {out}"\)
EOF)

Bash(python - <<'EOF'
from PIL import Image, ImageChops

src = r"C:\\Users\\Joe\\Desktop\\powermeter-demo\\powermeter-demo\\src\\assets\\logo.png"
img = Image.open\(src\).convert\("RGBA"\)

# Split out alpha; if no alpha, use white-background diff
r, g, b, a = img.split\(\)
# Create a white background version and diff to find non-white pixels
bg = Image.new\("RGBA", img.size, \(255, 255, 255, 255\)\)
diff = ImageChops.difference\(img.convert\("RGB"\), bg.convert\("RGB"\)\)
bbox = diff.getbbox\(\)
print\(f"Original: {img.size}, Content bbox: {bbox}"\)

if bbox:
    # Add a small padding of 8px
    pad = 8
    x1 = max\(0, bbox[0] - pad\)
    y1 = max\(0, bbox[1] - pad\)
    x2 = min\(img.width,  bbox[2] + pad\)
    y2 = min\(img.height, bbox[3] + pad\)
    cropped = img.crop\(\(x1, y1, x2, y2\)\)
    cropped.save\(src\)
    print\(f"Saved cropped: {cropped.size}"\)
EOF)
